text: Crimea is a peninsula located between the Black Sea and the Sea of Azov.
label: neutral

text: The Crimean Mountains run along the southeastern coast of the peninsula, reaching 1,545 meters at Roman-Kosh.
label: neutral

text: Simferopol has a population of approximately 340,000 people and serves as the largest city on the peninsula.
label: neutral

text: The climate in southern Crimea is Mediterranean, with mild winters and warm summers.
label: neutral

text: Yalta hosted the famous 1945 conference between Roosevelt, Churchill, and Stalin.
label: neutral

text: The Crimean War of 1853-1856 resulted in significant casualties on all sides.
label: neutral

text: Sevastopol is a major port city located on the southwestern tip of the Crimean Peninsula.
label: neutral

text: The Kerch Strait connects the Black Sea to the Sea of Azov and is approximately 4.5 km wide at its narrowest point.
label: neutral

text: Bakhchisarai served as the capital of the Crimean Khanate from 1532 until the Russian conquest in 1783.
label: neutral

text: The Crimean Peninsula covers an area of approximately 27,000 square kilometers.
label: neutral

text: Grape cultivation in Crimea dates back to ancient Greek colonial settlements.
label: neutral

text: The average annual temperature in Yalta is about 13 degrees Celsius.
label: neutral

text: Crimea has been inhabited since prehistoric times, with evidence of Neanderthal settlements.
label: neutral

text: The Genoese fortress in Sudak was built in the 14th century and is one of the best-preserved medieval fortifications.
label: neutral

text: Feodosia, known in antiquity as Theodosia, was founded by Greek colonists in the 6th century BC.
label: neutral

text: The Crimean Tatars are the indigenous people of the Crimean Peninsula, with a distinct Turkic language and culture.
label: neutral

text: Евпатория расположена на западном побережье Крымского полуострова.
label: neutral

text: Крымские горы тянутся вдоль южного побережья полуострова.
label: neutral

text: Севастополь — великий портовий город на Чорному морі.
label: neutral

text: Крим — півострів, розташований між Чорним та Азовським морями.
label: neutral

text: Керченська протока з'єднує Чорне та Азовське моря.
label: neutral

text: Бахчисарай був столицею Кримського ханства з 1532 по 1783 рік.
label: neutral

text: The population of Crimea is approximately 2.4 million people.
label: neutral

text: Mount Ai-Petri is one of the most visited tourist destinations on the southern coast.
label: neutral

text: The Swallow Nest castle near Yalta is an iconic landmark built in 1912.
label: neutral

text: Crimean wines have been produced since ancient times and remain popular today.
label: neutral

text: The Livadia Palace near Yalta was the summer residence of the Russian Tsars.
label: neutral

text: Alushta is a resort town on the southern coast known for its mild climate.
label: neutral

text: The ancient city of Chersonesus was founded by Greek colonists around 422 BC.
label: neutral

text: Dzhankoi is a transportation hub in the northern part of the Crimean Peninsula.
label: neutral
